title: European colonization
description: Practice your skills in this exercise on European colonization.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “They expressed their great satisfaction, saying that no greater good could come to them than to have our friendship, and that they desired to live in peace with their enemies, and that we should dwell in their land, in order that they might in the future more than ever before engage in hunting beavers, and give us a part of them in return for our providing them with things which they wanted . . . ”

-Samuel de Champlain, describing his interaction with a group of Algonquians, 1604 

**Which of the following was a tactic the French used to keep peaceful relations with Native Americans?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: French explorers married Native American women
French explorers created numerous colonies and gave Native Americans French citizenship
French explorers adopted orphaned Native American children
French explorers fought Dutch and English colonizers to help Native Americans keep their land

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “They expressed their great satisfaction, saying that no greater good could come to them than to have our friendship, and that they desired to live in peace with their enemies, and that we should dwell in their land, in order that they might in the future more than ever before engage in hunting beavers, and give us a part of them in return for our providing them with things which they wanted . . . ”

-Samuel de Champlain, describing his interaction with a group of Algonquians, 1604 

**Colonists from which of the following European nations would have been most likely to use similar tactics to those described in the excerpt above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Portugal
Spain
England
France

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “They expressed their great satisfaction, saying that no greater good could come to them than to have our friendship, and that they desired to live in peace with their enemies, and that we should dwell in their land, in order that they might in the future more than ever before engage in hunting beavers, and give us a part of them in return for our providing them with things which they wanted . . . ”

-Samuel de Champlain, describing his interaction with a group of Algonquians, 1604 

**How did the colonization efforts of the French and Dutch primarily differ from those of the Spanish?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The French and Dutch wanted to convert Native Americans to Christianity, while the Spanish did not
The French and Dutch wanted to use Native Americans as slave labor, while the Spanish did not
The French and Dutch had interracial marriages with Native Americans, while the Spanish did not
The French and Dutch relied heavily on trade alliances with Native Americans, while the Spanish did not

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below.

![Painting of European men in armor at war among two Native American groups](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/15ccee7b5eb37ae1ef774a43f87bbbd3.jpg)
Samuel de Champlain, engraving, 1609.

**In the above image, French explorer Samuel Champlain was most likely**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Joining with Algonquian tribes in fighting against their enemy, the Iroquois
Fighting to remove both Algonquian and Iroquois native peoples from eastern Canada
Demonstrating new hunting techniques to Native Americans in order to increase the number of beaver pelts they could sell
Attempting to put down the Pueblo Revolt

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below.

![Painting of European men in armor at war among two Native American groups](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/15ccee7b5eb37ae1ef774a43f87bbbd3.jpg)
Samuel de Champlain, engraving, 1609.

**The above image serves as evidence for which of the following historical arguments?**




[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Because French colonists were few in number, they generally had to adapt to Native American customs, unlike Spanish or British colonists
Because French colonists wore armor, they were less likely to die in battle than Spanish or British colonists
Because French colonists went to war against some tribes, they generally had worse relationships with Native Americans than British or Spanish colonists
Because French colonists traveled waterways by canoe, France controlled much larger territorial holdings in the Americas than the British or Spanish

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below.

![Painting of European men in armor at war among two Native American groups](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/15ccee7b5eb37ae1ef774a43f87bbbd3.jpg)
Samuel de Champlain, engraving, 1609.

**Which of the following statements explains why the French allied themselves with Native Americans?**




[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: French colonists sought to create permanent settlements and wanted positive relationships with large groups of Natives
French colonists sought interracial marriages to pass down immunities to major diseases to their children
French colonists were looking to make Native Americans citizens of France
French colonists were looking to establish fur-trading practices with Native Americans

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the map and answer the question below. 

![Map of New Amsterdam showing many ships on in the waters](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7747df82fb0e8acf17822e638a005f09.jpg)
Map of New Amsterdam, 1660. Image credit: [New-York Historical Society](https://www.nyhistory.org/web/crossroads/gallery/all/castello_plan_redraft.html)

**The above map demonstrates that Dutch colonization was chiefly focused on**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Maritime trade
Tobacco cultivation
Gold and silver mining
Sugar cultivation

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the map and answer the question below. 

![Map of New Amsterdam showing many ships on in the waters](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7747df82fb0e8acf17822e638a005f09.jpg)
Map of New Amsterdam, 1660. Image credit: [New-York Historical Society](https://www.nyhistory.org/web/crossroads/gallery/all/castello_plan_redraft.html)

**Which of the following statements describes the Dutch goal of settling New Amsterdam?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Location in the West Indies made conducting the Atlantic Slave Trade easier
Location near major mountain ranges made gold and silver mining easier
The environment and geography made an agricultural economy easier
Direct access to major ports and the Atlantic Ocean made trade easier

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the table and answer the question below.

Colony | European inhabitants by the year 1660
- | - 
New Netherland  |  5,000
New France  |  3,000
Chesapeake Bay  |  25,000
New England  |  33,000
From Alan Taylor, *American Colonies* (New York: Penguin Books, 2001).


**Which of the following factors was one cause of the trend depicted in the table above? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There were more "push" factors in England, such as poverty and religious persecution, than in France or the Netherlands
Potential settlers to the New World favored cold climates over tropical climates
French and Dutch settlers were more efficient at farming tobacco and therefore did not require a large population
There were fewer deadly diseases in the Chesapeake than in northern colonies

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the table and answer the question below.

Colony | European inhabitants by the year 1660
- | - 
New Netherland  |  5,000
New France  |  3,000
Chesapeake Bay  |  25,000
New England  |  33,000
From Alan Taylor, *American Colonies* (New York: Penguin Books, 2001).


**Which of the following statements explains a major difference in settlement patterns between French and English colonies in the 1600s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The French and Dutch only sent missionaries to convert Native Americans to Catholicism, resulting in less settlers
The French and Dutch sent less settlers because they were not focused on creating permanent settlements
The French and Dutch only allowed families to move to the Americas, resulting in less settlers
The French and Dutch settled at higher rates in an attempt to eradicate all Native Americans

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>Although the Country people be very barbarous, yet have they amongst them such government, as that their Magistrates for good commanding, and their people for due subjection, and obeying, excel many places that would be counted very civil. The form of their Common-wealth is a Monarchical government, one as Emperor ruleth over many Kings or Governours. Their chief ruler is called Powhatan, and taketh his name of his principal place of dwelling called Powhatan.

From John Smith, *The General History of Virginia, New England, and the Summer Isles* (London, 1624). 

**The excerpt would be most useful to historians as a source of information about which of the following?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The political differences between American Indians and English colonizers
The reasons for migration from England to the colonies
The conflicts English colonizers faced with American Indians
The ways in which English colonizers cooperated with American Indians for survival

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>Although the Country people be very barbarous, yet have they amongst them such government, as that their Magistrates for good commanding, and their people for due subjection, and obeying, excel many places that would be counted very civil. The form of their Common-wealth is a Monarchical government, one as Emperor ruleth over many Kings or Governours. Their chief ruler is called Powhatan, and taketh his name of his principal place of dwelling called Powhatan.

From John Smith, *The General History of Virginia, New England, and the Summer Isles* (London, 1624). 

**Which of the following statements most accurately illustrates the relationship early Jamestown settlers like Smith had with the Powhatans?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The early Jamestown settlers immediately went to war with the Powhatans after the kidnapping of John Smith
The early Jamestown settlers had small conflicts with the Powhatans over issues like settlers stealing food, but mostly cooperated with them
The early Jamestown settlers sought to enslave the Powhatans in an effort to cultivate tobacco
The early Jamestown settlers sought to create a trade network with the Powhatans to trade fur

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>You shall know that our Colonie consisteth now of seven hundred men at least, of sundry arts and professions, some more or less, they stand in health, and few sick. . . . The Colony is . . . strong and defensible by nature, a good air, wholesome and clear . . . with fresh and plenty of water springs, much fair and open grounds freed from woods, and wood enough at hand. . . . Here they have built competent and decent houses, the first story all of bricks, that every man may have his lodging and dwelling place apart by himself, with a sufficient quantity of ground allotted thereto for his orchard and garden to plant at his pleasure, and for his own use . . .

From “The New Life of Virginia,” a tract published in 1612 by the Virginia Company advertising the benefits of the colony.

**What was the original intention of the Virginia Company in founding the colony of Jamestown in 1607?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Founding a long-term English settlement in the New World
Providing a refuge for religious dissenters from Europe.
Quickly extracting natural resources from the Americas and making its investors wealthy
Establishing a fur-trading network with American Indians who lived in the area

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>You shall know that our Colonie consisteth now of seven hundred men at least, of sundry arts and professions, some more or less, they stand in health, and few sick. . . . The Colony is . . . strong and defensible by nature, a good air, wholesome and clear . . . with fresh and plenty of water springs, much fair and open grounds freed from woods, and wood enough at hand. . . . Here they have built competent and decent houses, the first story all of bricks, that every man may have his lodging and dwelling place apart by himself, with a sufficient quantity of ground allotted thereto for his orchard and garden to plant at his pleasure, and for his own use . . .

From “The New Life of Virginia,” a tract published in 1612 by the Virginia Company advertising the benefits of the colony.

**What kinds of settlers might the Virginia Company have hoped to attract to their colony with this tract?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Young, single men, looking to make their fortune
Catholics looking for religious freedom
Established families to build a permanent community
French traders to help negotiate with Native Americans

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>You shall know that our Colonie consisteth now of seven hundred men at least, of sundry arts and professions, some more or less, they stand in health, and few sick. . . . The Colony is . . . strong and defensible by nature, a good air, wholesome and clear . . . with fresh and plenty of water springs, much fair and open grounds freed from woods, and wood enough at hand. . . . Here they have built competent and decent houses, the first story all of bricks, that every man may have his lodging and dwelling place apart by himself, with a sufficient quantity of ground allotted thereto for his orchard and garden to plant at his pleasure, and for his own use . . .

From “The New Life of Virginia,” a tract published in 1612 by the Virginia Company advertising the benefits of the colony.

**What was the Virginia Company’s purpose for writing this tract?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Promote the ideals of republican government under the House of Burgesses
Advocate for the end of colonization of the Americas in an effort to avoid conflicts with Native Americans
Convince plantation owners to continue using indentured servants as the primary labor source over African slaves
Persuade men and women to migrate to the Americas or to invest in the colonization of Virginia
